title: Permission precheck
go_no_go_summary: Claude rated the S05 engineering deliverable GO with no P0/P1/P2 findings. Three P3 notes were optional informational observations and explicitly deferred because the substantive S05 guarantees are preserved by the inherited S04 input gate, the S05 strict fail-closed truthy-claim loop, result validator false-flag checks, and no-write/no-runtime/no-AuthZ boundaries.

findings.title: Permission decision ref and wrong marker negative paths are transitive
findings.summary: Claude noted that missing permission_decision_ref and wrong upstream marker failures are caught by the inherited S04 input validator before S05-specific logic runs.
findings.resolution: Deferred as intentional defense-in-depth. S05 formally consumes a validated S04 Matter trace result and still records requiresPermissionDecisionRef, fail_closed_on missing_permission_decision_ref, and result-validator nonblank ref checks.

findings.title: Entrypoint model effort read-only checks are transitive
findings.summary: Claude noted that S05 reasserts entrypoint, model, effort, and read-only fields already guaranteed by the S04 result validator.
findings.resolution: Deferred as harmless defense-in-depth on a security boundary. S05 carries already-validated review metadata and does not add runtime authority.

findings.title: S05 models denials as throw-only rather than soft deny result states
findings.summary: Claude noted that S05 decision_values contains allow_metadata_only and blocked_claim_refs is always empty, unlike S04 soft denial result states.
findings.resolution: Deferred as intentional for this permission precheck slice. Unsafe or denied states fail closed by throwing before a production_ready result is emitted; deny-path observability can be reconsidered in a later routing or blocked-claim output subphase.

invalid_partial_attempts.reason: Earlier attempts before the unrestricted environment change could not authenticate or could not start the OAuth callback server; they produced no valid review verdict and are not counted as Claude review runs.

claude_cli.command: claude -p --add-dir /tmp --model claude-opus-4-8 --effort max --permission-mode dontAsk --no-session-persistence --tools Read --disallowedTools Bash,Edit,Write,MultiEdit,NotebookEdit,Grep,Glob --output-format json

findings.title: Transitive permission decision ref guard observability
findings.summary: Missing permission_decision_ref and wrong upstream marker paths are caught by inherited S04 validation before S05-specific logic.

findings.title: Transitive entrypoint model effort read-only checks
findings.summary: S05 repeats review metadata checks already guaranteed by S04; this remains defense-in-depth.

findings.title: Throw-only denial semantics
findings.summary: S05 emits allow_metadata_only or throws rather than representing soft deny result states.

required_followups: Continue with RP00.P02.M04.S06 Audit hint precheck as the next subphase.
review_notes: Claude confirmed S05 consumes a validated S04 Matter trace result and preserves S01, S02, S03, S04, S20, and permission/audit baseline dependencies.
Claude confirmed denied S04 Matter trace results fail closed before S05 emits a permission result.
Claude confirmed missing permission_decision_ref fails closed through inherited validation and S05 result validation.
Claude confirmed runtime permission engine, AuthZ evaluator, runtime Claude review, audit write, review queue write, service execution, runtime route, and product-state write claims are rejected.
Claude confirmed RP00.P02.M04 stays open and hands off to RP00.P02.M04.S06.
parsed_review.summary: Claude reported GO and no P0/P1/P2 issues. P3 notes are optional hardening or inherited-gate observations.

raw_claude_cli_result_summary.summary: Claude returned GO with no P0/P1/P2 findings and three P3 optional informational notes.
